steps.title: Vertex shortcut: find the axis of symmetry
Use quadratic regression as a Desmos shortcut
steps.content: To make Desmos display the vertex form, enter three easy points from the quadratic and fit $y_1\sim a(x_1-h)^2+k$. The fitted values are $a=-6$, $h=5$, and $k=24$, so the vertex is $(5,24)$. Because $a<0$, that vertex gives the maximum value.

steps.title: Read the maximum directly from vertex form
steps.content: Vertex form is $a(x-h)^2+k$, and the maximum value is the constant $k$ when the parabola opens downward. Here the equivalent form is $-6(x-5)^2+24$, so the maximum value is displayed as $24$. That matches choice C.
